title: Compliance — Conflict of Interest (COI)
content:
Morgan State University's Conflict of Interest (COI) program covers two distinct but related streams: (A) COI in Sponsored Research — Financial Conflicts of Interest (FCOI) in federally funded research, governed by Public Health Service regulations at 42 CFR Part 50 Subpart F and National Science Foundation conflict-of-interest policies. FCOIs are defined as financial interests that could directly and significantly affect the design, conduct, or reporting of research. All investigators must complete CITI conflict-of-interest training, refreshed every four years, and must disclose significant financial interests before any sponsored research is undertaken. The Office of Research Compliance establishes procedures for managing, reducing, or eliminating significant financial conflicts. Disclosure is filed via the Financial Conflict of Interest Form (Google Form at docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeQQps5_dzvY75zyY88epYD0foyK2Fbt9qXxXnDSrloTt45LA/viewform). (B) COI in Research and Development, Technology Transfer, and Commercialization — In 2017, the Morgan State University Board of Regents approved a Conflict of Interest Policy in Research and Development to align with other Maryland research universities and promote economic development. The policy is anchored in the Maryland Public Ethics Law and the Public-Private Partnership Act for Institutions of Higher Education. It enables exemptions from certain state ethics law provisions for university personnel engaged in R&D activities, collaborative research, intellectual-property commercialization, and technical assistance. A nine-member Conflict of Interest Committee (COIC), chaired by Dr. Willie May (Vice President for Research and Economic Development), reviews faculty exemption requests for research, economic-development, and start-up activities. COIC members include Dr. Lorece Edwards, Dr. Simone Gibson, Dr. Mansoureh Jeihani, Dr. Pumtiwitt McCarthy, Dr. Gregory Ramsey, Mr. Wayne Swann, Mr. Paul Voos, and Ms. Sharon Whitehurst. Faculty resources cover: the Board of Regents Policy on COI in Research and Development, the Procedures on Conflict of Interest and Professional Commitment of Faculty (PDF), the COI in R&D Disclosure Form (Word), the COI Process Description (PDF), the COI Flow Chart (PDF), the COI Management Plan Example (Word), guidelines for Private Company Contracts (PDF), guidelines on Involvement in Start-ups (PDF), a COI Educational Slide Deck (PowerPoint), the COI Training Module 2025 (PowerPoint), and the State Public Ethics Law (PDF). The Office of Technology Transfer (OTT) administers the R&D-stream COI program. OTT is located at Earl S. Richardson Library, Suite 207, 1700 E. Cold Spring Lane, Baltimore, MD 21251, and can be reached at 443-885-1003. General sponsored-research COI inquiries go to the Office of Research Administration at ask.ora@morgan.edu or 443-885-4044.

=== PLAYWRIGHT RE-VERIFICATION 2026-05-14 — Page Structure Audit ===
The live `conflict-of-interest-x12058` URL is a SLIM HUB landing page (no accordions, ~200 words of orientation copy). It exposes only two outbound links: (1) `/research-compliance/conflict-of-interest/conflict-of-interest-for-sponsored-research` (FCOI subpage), and (2) `https://www.morgan.edu/technology-transfer-and-intellectual-property/conflict-of-interest` (Office of Technology Transfer COI in R&D page, which 301-redirects to `/technology-transfer-and-intellectual-property/conflict-of-interest-in-randd`). All detailed content in THIS canonical doc was sourced from those two subpages.

=== Newly-revealed FCOI accordion details (from Sponsored Research subpage, captured 2026-05-14) ===
The FCOI subpage exposes 3 accordions:
- Procedures → MSU 'FCOI Disclosure Process and Management' PDF at https://www.morgan.edu/Documents/ADMINISTRATION/OFFICES/ora/COI/MorganStateUniversity_FCOIDisclosureProcessandManagement.pdf
- Policy → 'MSU Board of Regents Conflict of Interest in Research and Development Policy' PDF at https://www.morgan.edu/Documents/ADMINISTRATION/OFFICES/ora/COI/MorganStateUniversity_ConflictOfInterest_Policy.pdf (NOTE: this is a SECOND, ORA-hosted copy of the BOR COI Policy distinct from the OTT-hosted version)
- Disclosure Form → Financial Conflict of Interest Google Form (URL already captured above).
The FCOI subpage also explicitly defines covered investigators as Investigator + spouse/domestic partner + dependent children, and reiterates the 4-year CITI training cycle.

=== Investigator scope detail (newly captured) ===
Financial interests covered by FCOI policy include those of the Investigator AND those of the Investigator's spouse/domestic partner and/or dependent children. Many aspects of research — design, data collection, analysis, reporting, supplier selection — can be inappropriately influenced by such interests.

For per-subpage canonical detail, see compliance_coi_hub_x12058, compliance_coi_sponsored_research.
